name: tenacity
summary: Retry any Python function with exponential backoff, jitter, and custom stop conditions — three decorator lines replace 30 lines of retry logic.

story.problem: External APIs fail. Network calls time out. Databases hiccup. Without retry logic, your application crashes on the first transient failure. Writing retry loops by hand is repetitive, error-prone, and hard to configure correctly.
story.mental_model: tenacity decorates your function with retry behavior. You declare when to stop (max attempts, timeout), how to wait between attempts (fixed, exponential, random), and what to retry on (exception types, return values). The decorator handles the loop, logging, and re-raising.
story.when_to_use: Any I/O that can fail transiently: HTTP calls, database queries, queue operations, cloud API calls, file system operations on network drives.
story.when_not_to_use: Don't retry non-idempotent operations (POST that creates resources) without careful thought. Don't retry errors that won't resolve with time (auth failures, 400 Bad Request).

nodes.label: @retry
nodes.summary: Main decorator — wraps a function with retry logic

nodes.label: wait_exponential()
nodes.summary: Exponential backoff — doubles wait each attempt

nodes.label: retry_if_exception_type()
nodes.summary: Only retry on specific exception types

nodes.label: before_sleep callback
nodes.summary: Hook called before each sleep — good for logging

tasks.title: Retry an API call with exponential backoff

steps.label: Basic retry decorator
Async retry
steps.code:
from tenacity import retry, stop_after_attempt, wait_random_exponential, before_sleep_log
import logging
import asyncio
import httpx

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

@retry(
    stop=stop_after_attempt(5),
    wait=wait_random_exponential(min=1, max=30),  # jitter prevents thundering herd
    before_sleep=before_sleep_log(logger, logging.WARNING),
)
async def fetch_async(url: str) -> dict:
    async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
        r = await client.get(url, timeout=10)
        r.raise_for_status()
        return r.json()
